content: Bollinger Bands assume normal distribution of returns, which financial markets violate (fat tails). Price touching bands doesn't reliably signal reversal; in strong trends, price can 'walk the bands' indefinitely. The 2-standard-deviation setting captures ~95% of data only if returns are normal, which they aren't. Band squeezes don't consistently predict breakouts; false squeezes are common. Bands provide descriptive volatility measurement but limited predictive value.
